owned_files.notes: 9-column schema (id, name, category, subcategory, base_material, weight_kg, stack_size, durability, description). Categories now include: clothing, tool, weapon, furniture, food, material, machine, vehicle, medical, electronics, container, decoration. Recent expansion added jewelry, books/scrolls/writing, musical instruments, art supplies, lighting, cooked foods, fishing/camping gear, plumbing/sanitation, and specialty materials. Effectively at the 500-item milestone.

10-column schema (id, name, category, inputs, outputs, craft_time_sec, station_required, skill_required, skill_level, description). 9 categories: smelting, refining, crafting, construction, textile, cooking, electronics, assembly, chemistry. Recent expansion added jewelry forging, book/writing crafting, candle/lantern/flashlight assembly, cooked-food recipes (soup, stew, sandwich, pasta, pizza, omelette, cake, pie, cookies), hygiene/plumbing chemistry and casting, instrument crafting, and outdoor/camping kit. Significantly above the 200-recipe milestone.

gaps: Subcategory diversity is healthy but late-game spacecraft components could use more depth
No cross-validation that every recipe input/output id exists in items.csv (some recipes reference brass_ingot_0 / plant_fiber_0 ids that may need their own item rows)
Category 'decoration' is now overloaded as the home for jewelry and instruments; future work could split into dedicated categories
summary: Items grew from 353 to 505 rows (+152), reaching the 500-item milestone. Recipes grew from 307 to 429 rows (+122), continuing well past the 200-recipe minimum. Expansion focused on previously thin categories: jewelry, books/scrolls/writing, musical instruments, art supplies, lighting, cooked foods, fishing/camping gear, plumbing/sanitation, and specialty raw materials. Recommended status remains active to keep iterating on subcategory depth and cross-validation, even though the headline milestone is met.
